reforms.description: The 1976 Budget increased the share of public capital expenditure devoted to capital grants and loans to industry, reaching an all-time high of 26 percent, aimed at stimulating industrial growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for capital grants and loans to industry is expected to stimulate industrial growth and create jobs.
reforms.status_evidence: The share of total public capital expenditure devoted to capital grants and loans to industry is budgeted to reach an all-time high.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in government priorities towards supporting industrial growth through increased capital investment, which is crucial for long-term economic development.
reforms.source_quote: In 1976 this share is budgeted to reach an all-time high of 26 per cent compared with the previous peak of 21 per cent in 1971.
